page.content: In Fiscal Year 2024, Siemens Healthineers reports robust Diagnostic Test Volume Growth, with Q1FY2024 achieving a percentage change of 15% and a P/E Ratio of 25.3, yielding a Current Ratio of 1.8.

lines.text: In Fiscal Year 2024, Siemens Healthineers reports robust Diagnostic Test Volume Growth, with Q1FY2024 achieving a
percentage change of 15% and a P/E Ratio of 25.3, yielding a Current Ratio of 1.8.

page.content: Q2FY2024 demonstrates a stronger growth of 20%, with a P/E Ratio of 26.1 and a Current Ratio of 1.9. In Q3FY2024, the percentage change slightly declines to 18%, accompanied by a P/E Ratio of 27.0 and a Current Ratio of 2.0.

lines.text: Q2FY2024 demonstrates a stronger growth of 20%, with a P/E Ratio of 26.1 and a Current Ratio of 1.9. In Q3FY2024, the
percentage change slightly declines to 18%, accompanied by a P/E Ratio of 27.0 and a Current Ratio of 2.0.

page.content: Q4FY2024 reflects the highest growth at 22%, with a P/E Ratio of 28.5 and a Current Ratio of 2.1. The Clinical Staffing Ratios highlight varying service metrics across departments: Emergency Care has a Nurses Ratio of 1:4 and a Doctors Ratio of 1:10, generating Total Revenue of £15M, an Operating Income of £5M, CAPEX of £1.2M, and a Growth Rate of 8.0%. Surgery shows a Nurses Ratio of 1:3 and a Doctors Ratio of 1:8, with Total Revenue of £20M, Operating Income of £6M, CAPEX of £1.5M, and a 10.0% growth. Pediatrics reports a Nurses Ratio of 1:5, a Doctors Ratio of 1:12, Total Revenue of £12M, Operating Income of £4M, CAPEX of £0.8M, and 7.5% growth. Oncology, Cardiology, Geriatrics, and Rehabilitation follow with respective metrics, reflecting diverse performance across services.
